Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Consensus Board Game 23 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Afroman Wins Civil Trial over Use of Police Raid Footage in His Music Videos 237 upvotes, 27 comments
  3. OpenRocket 668 upvotes, 114 comments
  4. Autoresearch for SAT Solvers 150 upvotes, 30 comments
  5. Wander – A tiny, decentralised tool to explore the small web 318 upvotes, 78 comments
  6. Nvidia greenboost: transparently extend GPU VRAM using system RAM/NVMe 424 upvotes, 118 comments
  7. Conway's Game of Life, in real life 253 upvotes, 66 comments
  8. Pretraining Language Models via Neural Cellular Automata 47 upvotes, 11 comments
  9. Afroman found not liable in defamation case 673 upvotes, 273 comments
  10. A Preview of Coalton 0.2 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  11. Show HN: Duplicate 3 layers in a 24B LLM, logical deduction .22→.76. No training 191 upvotes, 71 comments
  12. Juggalo Makeup Blocks Facial Recognition Technology (2019) 96 upvotes, 32 comments
  13. How many branches can your CPU predict? 53 upvotes, 15 comments
  14. OpenBSD: PF queues break the 4 Gbps barrier 60 upvotes, 19 comments
  15. The next fight over the use of facial recognition could be in the supermarkets 29 upvotes, 7 comments
  16. Astral to Join OpenAI 455 upvotes, 237 comments
  17. A sufficiently detailed spec is code 502 upvotes, 265 comments
  18. Gluon: Explicit Performance 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  19. The Shape of Inequalities 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  20. OpenAI to Acquire Astral 153 upvotes, 77 comments
  21. LotusNotes 144 upvotes, 74 comments
  22. Eniac, the First General-Purpose Digital Computer, Turns 80 71 upvotes, 32 comments
  23. Warranty Void If Regenerated 453 upvotes, 275 comments
  24. The math that explains why bell curves are everywhere 177 upvotes, 107 comments
  25. 'Your Frustration Is the Product' 188 upvotes, 115 comments
  26. Nvidia NemoClaw 350 upvotes, 230 comments
  27. Twelve-Tone Composition 22 upvotes, 8 comments
  28. Stdwin: Standard window interface by Guido Van Rossum [pdf] 62 upvotes, 36 comments
  29. 2% of ICML papers desk rejected because the authors used LLM in their reviews 147 upvotes, 131 comments
  30. Austin’s surge of new housing construction drove down rents 667 upvotes, 804 comments

Last generated 11:30pm, 19 March 2026. Source here.
